paras.text: Project submit karne ke baad, dono canteen me baithe thak ke chai pee rahe the.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Yaar tumhe kabhi laga hai ki hum dono milke kuch bada karein? Jaise ek YouTube channel jahan English sikhaye jaaye?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (aankhein chamkate hue) Wow, ye idea to mast hai! Socho zara — agar hum channel shuru karein, hazaaron students ki madad ho sakti hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Haan na! But abhi to bas ek sapna hai, hai na? Isko English me kaise bolenge — jab kuch abhi real nahi hai, sirf imagination hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul sahi pakda tumne, ye alag category hai — Type 1 se alag. Type 1 me humne 'real, possible' baatein ki thi, jaise 'agar hum time pe kaam karenge, jeetenge' — wo ho sakta hai easily. But 'agar hum channel shuru karein' — abhi to koi plan hi nahi hai, bas sapna hai. Iske liye use hota hai If we started a channel, we would help thousands of students!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, structure kya hai iska? Type 1 se kaise alag hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Dekho fark — Type 1 me 'if' clause me PRESENT simple tha, yahan 'if' clause me PAST simple aata hai — 'If we started...' Aur main clause me 'will' ki jagah 'would' aata hai — 'we would help...' Bas structure ka pattern ek step peeche shift ho jaata hai, isse hi pata chalta hai ki ye hypothetical/imaginary hai, real nahi.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ruko, confuse ho gaya — 'started' to past tense hai na? Matlab past ki baat ho rahi hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Yahi sabse bada confusion hota hai sabko! Nahi, yahan 'started' PAST TENSE ki baat nahi kar raha — ye sirf GRAMMAR ka form hai jo dikhata hai ki baat UNREAL/IMAGINARY hai, chahe wo present ya future ki hi baat kyun na ho. Time abhi bhi present/future hi hai, bas form badal gaya hai ye batane ke liye ki ye sirf socha ja raha hai, real nahi hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ohh, matlab past tense yahan 'distance' dikhata hai reality se, time ki baat nahi kar raha?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi samjhe! Ye ek achha tareeka hai yaad rakhne ka — 'past form = distance from reality', chahe time koi bhi ho. Chalo aur examples dekhte hain taaki pakka clear ho jaaye.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Haan bolo, main sun raha hoon.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ek classic example — 'Agar mere paas bahut paise hote, main duniya ghoomta.' Ye kabhi ho sakta hai future me, but ABHI ke liye sirf ek sapna/wish hai, isliye Type 2 — 'If I had a lot of money, I would travel the world.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, aur agar bilkul hi impossible baat ho, jaise 'agar mere pankh hote'? Wo bhi Type 2 hi hoga?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Haan bilkul, wo bhi Type 2 hi hai — 'If I had wings, I would fly.' Bas dhyan rakho, Type 2 dono cover karta hai — jo THEORETICALLY possible hai but abhi real nahi (jaise paise wala example), aur jo COMPLETELY impossible hai (jaise pankh wala example). Dono ek hi structure use karte hain.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ek cheez poochni hai — maine kahin suna tha 'was' ki jagah 'were' use karte hain is type me, sach hai kya?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Haan, bahut important rule hai ye! Jab 'be' verb use karna ho Type 2 me, to sab subjects ke saath — I, he, she, it — hamesha 'WERE' use hota hai, 'was' nahi. Jaise 'Agar main teacher hoti, main sabko free me sikhati' — 'If I WERE a teacher, I would teach everyone for free.' 'I was' nahi, 'I were' — bas isi jagah exception hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook me likhte hue) Ye bahut important lag raha hai, note kar liya — Type 2 me 'were' hamesha, chahe subject koi bhi ho. Aur bahut famous phrase bhi hai na 'if I were you'?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul! 'If I were you, I would apologize first' — advice dene ka bahut common tareeka hai ye, jaise Hindi me bolte hain 'main tumhari jagah hota to pehle sorry bol deta.' Bahut useful phrase hai roz ki English me.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, waise main to abhi teacher nahi hoon, but agar hota, tumhe kabhi daant nahi padti grammar ki galtiyon pe.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hasm ke) Sapno me raho tum, reality me main hi tumhari teacher hoon! Waise dekho, tumne khud hi Type 2 use kar liya sentence me — 'agar tum teacher hote' — perfect practice.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Sach me? Chalo ab main try karta hoon khud se banane ka — 'Agar humara channel successful hota, hum college chhod dete.'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hasm ke) Arey itni jaldi college chhodne ki baat mat karo! But grammar sahi hai — 'If our channel were successful, we would leave college.' Bas thoda practical socho pehle.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Achha theek hai, ek aur try karta hoon, thoda realistic wala — 'Agar hum roz ek video banayein, hum jaldi grow karenge.'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Hmm, ruko — ye to Type 1 jaisa lag raha hai, kyunki roz video banana ekdum possible hai, koi imaginary baat nahi! Isko 'If we make a video daily, we will grow quickly' bolenge — Type 1, present simple aur will.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ohh! Matlab mujhe pehle decide karna padega ki baat REALLY possible hai (Type 1) ya sirf IMAGINARY/hypothetical hai (Type 2), tabhi sahi structure choose kar paunga?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi! Yahi sabse important skill hai conditionals me — pehle socho 'kya ye sach me ho sakta hai easily (Type 1), ya sirf ek sapna/hypothetical baat hai (Type 2)?' Uske baad hi structure decide karo.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha ek last cheez — kya main clause me 'would' ki jagah kuch aur bhi aa sakta hai, jaise Type 1 me 'might/can' aaya tha?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Haan bilkul, same tarike se — 'could' aata hai jab ability/possibility ki baat ho, aur 'might' aata hai jab thoda kam confident possibility ho. Jaise 'If I had more time, I could help you' (ability), ya 'If it were sunny, we might go for a walk' (kam confident possibility).

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sochte hue) Achha, matlab 'would' sabse strong/confident hai, 'could' ability dikhata hai, aur 'might' sabse kam confident hai — teeno alag shades hain same imaginary situation ke.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Perfect summary! Dekha, kitna kuch seekh liya ek chai peete peete.
Interview hall ke bahar, saalon baad, wahi ladki soch rahi thi — kya sach me wo channel wala sapna kabhi pura hoga?

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'If I was rich, I will buy a big house.'
quiz.answer: If I were rich, I would buy a big house.
quiz.explain: Type 2 Conditional me 'if' clause me Past Simple (aur 'be' verb ke liye hamesha 'were') aata hai, aur main clause me 'would', 'will' nahi.

quiz.q: Fill (hypothetical/unreal present): If she ___ (know) the answer, she ___ (tell) us.
quiz.explain: Unreal present situation — If + Past Simple, Subject + would + V1.

quiz.q: Type 1 vs Type 2 — Which sentence shows something purely imaginary, not really possible right now? a) If it rains, I will stay home. b) If I had wings, I would fly.
quiz.answer: b) If I had wings, I would fly.
quiz.explain: Pankh hona ek impossible/hypothetical baat hai, isliye Type 2 (If + past, would) fit baithta hai; option a) ek real future possibility hai (Type 1).

quiz.q: Fill using the special 'be' verb rule: 'If I ___ you, I would think twice before deciding.'
quiz.explain: Type 2 Conditional me 'be' verb ke liye sab subjects (I/he/she/it included) ke saath hamesha 'were' use hota hai, 'was' nahi — ye ek fixed exception hai.

quiz.q: Decide Type 1 or Type 2: 'Agar kal baarish hui, hum picnic cancel kar denge.' (This is a realistic possibility for tomorrow.)
quiz.answer: Type 1 — If it rains tomorrow, we will cancel the picnic.
quiz.explain: Kal baarish hona ek real, possible future event hai — koi imaginary/hypothetical baat nahi, isliye Type 1 (present simple + will) sahi hai, Type 2 nahi.

quiz.q: Fill with the right modal for a less-confident possibility: 'If the weather were nice, we ___ (might/would definitely) go for a walk.' Which shows LESS certainty?
quiz.explain: 'Would' sabse confident/definite result dikhata hai, 'could' ability dikhata hai, aur 'might' sabse kam confident possibility dikhata hai — teeno Type 2 ke main clause me use ho sakte hain, matlab thoda alag hota hai.

quiz.q: Translate to English (Type 2): 'Agar main uski jagah hota, main pehle sorry bolta.'
quiz.answer: If I were in his place, I would apologize first. (or: If I were him, I would apologize first.)
quiz.explain: Hypothetical advice/imagination dene ka classic Type 2 pattern — 'If I were...' bahut common phrase hai advice dete waqt.
